name: bpf
description: Zig-native eBPF framework вҖ” write, compile, load, and manage BPF programs entirely in Zig. Includes kernel-side helpers, ELF loader with CO-RE support, typed maps, skeleton code generation, vmlinux BTF codegen, and a QEMU-based multi-architecture test harness.
readme:
# bpf: BPF package for Zig

NOTE: This package is almost entirely LLM generated. I've been building out
testing and the different features and it is under review.

A Zig-native eBPF framework for writing, loading, and managing BPF programs
entirely in Zig. Covers the full BPF lifecycle: compile kernel-side BPF
programs with Zig's BPF backend, load into the kernel, manage maps and
attachments from userspace.

## Features

### Kernel-side (`kern`)
- 100+ BPF helper function declarations
- Map declarations: HashMap, ArrayMap, PerfEventArray, RingBuf, ProgArray, DevMap, CpuMap, SockMap, LpmTrie, ArrayOfMaps, HashOfMaps, and more
- Section naming helpers for all program types (kprobe, uprobe, xdp, tc, socket, fentry, etc.)
- Context structs: XdpMd, SkBuff, BpfSock, BpfTcpSock, BpfSockOps
- Portable pt_regs access: PtArgs (comptime), PtArgsPortable (load-time patching), ctx_arg (explicit)
- CO-RE field access: core_read / core_read_ptr with patchable .core_relo section
- Network protocol headers: Ethernet, IPv4, IPv6, TCP, UDP, ICMP, ARP

### ELF Loader (`elf`)
- Parse BPF ELF objects, discover maps, programs, and global data sections
- Map relocation patching (BPF_PSEUDO_MAP_FD, BPF_PSEUDO_MAP_VALUE)
- .rodata/.data/.bss data section maps with merged constant pools
- BTF loading into kernel, BTF.ext func_info and line_info
- CO-RE relocations: all 16 relocation kinds (field, type, enum, ksym, kconfig)
- Map-in-map support (array_of_maps, hash_of_maps) with auto prototype creation
- Map pinning, reuse, and data section overrides
- Verifier log capture
- ksym relocations: typed (BPF_PSEUDO_BTF_ID) and typeless (kallsyms)

### Skeleton (build-step code generation)
- `gen-skeleton` build tool: parses BPF ELF, extracts raw instruction .bin files
- No ELF parsing at runtime вҖ” programs loaded from pre-extracted data
- Data section merging and relocation patching
- `add_bpf_skeleton()` build integration вҖ” returns an importable module

### Typed Maps (`typed_map`)
- `Map(K, V)`, `PerCpuMap(K, V)`, `MapOfMaps(K)`, `LpmTrie`, `ProgArray`, `DevMap`, `CpuMap`, `SockMap`, `SockHash`, `XskMap`, `Queue(V)`, `Stack(V)`, `BloomFilter(V)`, `BatchOps`

### BTF / CO-RE
- BTF wire format parser and encoder
- BTF.ext parser with CO-RE relocation records
- CO-RE relocation engine: field offset/size/exists, type matching, enum values, ksym/kconfig
- BTF-to-Zig codegen (`gen-vmlinux`): generate typed Zig structs from kernel BTF
- Vendored `vmlinux.zig` for typed kernel struct access
- Verifier log annotator with source location mapping

### Userspace Utilities
- kconfig reader: parse /proc/config.gz, patch .kconfig sections
- kallsyms reader: parse /proc/kallsyms for kernel symbol addresses
- Attach helpers: kprobe, uprobe, tracepoint, XDP, TC
- Ring buffer and perf buffer consumers
- Link management, trace pipe reader, feature probing

### Testing Infrastructure
- QEMU test harness: boot real kernels, run BPF programs via virtio-serial RPC
- 5 architecture support: x86_64, aarch64, riscv64, s390x, powerpc64
- prog_test_run based tests for socket_filter, XDP, and TC programs
- Forgejo CI with kernel caching and matrix strategy

## Quick Start

### 1. Add the dependency

```sh
zig fetch --save 'git+https://codeberg.org/mattnite/bpf'
```

### 2. Write a BPF program

```zig
// src/my_prog.bpf.zig
const kern = @import("kern");
comptime { _ = kern.license("GPL"); }

const pkt_count = kern.ArrayMap("pkt_count", u64, 1);

export fn xdp_count(ctx: ?*kern.XdpMd) linksection(kern.SEC.xdp) callconv(.c) c_int {
    _ = ctx;
    const key: u32 = 0;
    if (kern.map_lookup_elem(&pkt_count.value, @ptrCast(&key))) |val| {
        const ptr: *u64 = @ptrCast(@alignCast(val));
        ptr.* += 1;
    }
    return 2; // XDP_PASS
}
```

### 3. Load and run

```zig
const elf = @import("elf");

pub fn main() !void {
    var obj = try elf.BpfObject.open(allocator, @embedFile("my_prog.o"));
    defer obj.close();
    try obj.load();

    const prog_fd = obj.find_program("xdp_count") orelse return error.NotFound;
    const map_fd = obj.find_map("pkt_count") orelse return error.NotFound;
    // attach to interface, read map counters, etc.
    _ = prog_fd;
    _ = map_fd;
}
```

### 4. Or use the skeleton (no ELF parsing at runtime)

```zig
// build.zig:
const bpf_obj = addBpfObject(b, .{ .name = "my_prog", .source = b.path("src/my_prog.bpf.zig") });
const skel = add_bpf_skeleton(b, "my_prog", bpf_obj);
my_app.root_module.addImport("my_prog", skel);
```

```zig
// userspace:
const skel = @import("my_prog");
var s = try skel.rt.load(allocator, &skel.map_defs, &skel.map_names,
    &skel.prog_defs, &skel.relocs, &skel.data_section_defs, skel.license);
defer s.destroy();
```

## CO-RE (Compile Once вҖ” Run Everywhere)

Portable kernel struct access across kernel versions without compiler
builtins. Field offsets stored in a patchable `.core_relo` section:

```zig
const vmlinux = @import("vmlinux");
const kern = @import("kern");

const TaskOffsets = extern struct {
    pid: u32 = @offsetOf(vmlinux.task_struct, "pid"),
    tgid: u32 = @offsetOf(vmlinux.task_struct, "tgid"),
    real_parent: u32 = @offsetOf(vmlinux.task_struct, "real_parent"),
};
export var task: TaskOffsets linksection(".core_relo") = .{};

export fn probe(ctx: ?*anyopaque) linksection(kern.SEC.kprobe("do_nanosleep")) callconv(.c) c_int {
    _ = ctx;
    const t: *const anyopaque = @ptrFromInt(kern.get_current_task_btf());
    const pid = kern.core_read(i32, t, &task.pid);
    const parent = kern.core_read_ptr(t, &task.real_parent) orelse return -1;
    const ppid = kern.core_read(i32, parent, &task.tgid);
    _ = pid;
    _ = ppid;
    return 0;
}
```

See `docs/core-guide.md` for the full CO-RE guide.

## vmlinux.zig Generation

Generate typed Zig structs from the running kernel's BTF:

```sh
# On a Linux machine:
zig build gen-vmlinux -- --btf /sys/kernel/btf/vmlinux -o src/vmlinux.zig

# Filter to specific types:
zig build gen-vmlinux -- --btf /tmp/vmlinux.btf --types task_struct,file,inode -o src/vmlinux.zig
```

## Building and Testing

### Unit tests

```sh
zig build test
```

### QEMU integration tests

Run BPF programs in real kernels via QEMU:

```sh
# All tests in a single QEMU boot:
zig build test-all-qemu -Dkernel=aarch64-Image -Darch=aarch64

# Individual test suites:
zig build test-prog-exercises-qemu -Dkernel=aarch64-Image -Darch=aarch64
zig build test-map-in-map-qemu -Dkernel=aarch64-Image -Darch=aarch64
zig build test-core-field-qemu -Dkernel=aarch64-Image -Darch=aarch64
zig build test-skeleton-load-qemu -Dkernel=aarch64-Image -Darch=aarch64
zig build test-vmlinux-pt-regs-qemu -Dkernel=aarch64-Image -Darch=aarch64
zig build test-kconfig-qemu -Dkernel=aarch64-Image -Darch=aarch64
zig build test-verifier-log-qemu -Dkernel=aarch64-Image -Darch=aarch64
```

## Architecture

```

в”Ӯ                   BPF Programs                       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  kern.zig  net.zig  vmlinux.zig                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  (freestanding вҖ” compiles to bpfel/bpfeb target)     в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ              Build Tools (run at build time)          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  gen-skeleton  gen-vmlinux                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  skeleton_runtime.zig  btf_codegen.zig               в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ               ELF Loader / CO-RE                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  elf.zig  btf.zig  btf_ext.zig  core_relo.zig       в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ                   Userspace API                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  typed_map.zig  attach.zig  link.zig                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  ring_buffer.zig  perf_buffer.zig  kconfig.zig       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  kallsyms.zig  features.zig  trace.zig               в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ                   Syscall Layer                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  user.zig  sys.zig                                   в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```
